title: चंद मिनटों की बारिश, फिर पहले जैसे लू... - Dainik Bhaskar
full_text:
चंद मिनटों की बारिश, फिर पहले जैसे लू...
- कॉपी लिंक
अमृतसर | शहर में बुधवार सुबह कई जगह रुक-रुक कर हुई बारिश हुई। इस चंद मिनटों की बारिश ने कुछ समय के लिए मौसम सुहावना जरूर किया, लेकिन इसके तुरंत बाद धूप निकलने से मौसम फिर पहले जैसा हो गया और तेज लू व भारी उमस ने लोगों को दिनभर बेहाल रखा। बुधवार को अध
मौसम विभाग के अनुसार 25 और 26 जून को आंशिक रूप से बादल छाए रहने तथा एक-दो स्थानों पर हल्की बारिश या गरज-चमक के साथ बौछारें पड़ने की संभावना है। इसके बाद 27 जून से मौसम अपेक्षाकृत साफ रहने का अनुमान है। विभाग का कहना है कि फिलहाल वातावरण में नमी अधिक होने के कारण लोगों को उमस महसूस होती रहेगी। 28 जून से 30 जून के बीच अधिकतम तापमान बढ़कर 39 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंच सकता है। ऐसे में महीने के अंतिम दिनों में गर्मी फिर से अपना असर दिखा सकती है।

title: France vs Paraguay: Extreme Heat Threat at World Cup Last-16 - worldofsports.io

full_text:
France head to Philadelphia’s Lincoln Financial Field for a World Cup last-16 clash with Paraguay under a severe heat warning, with Didier Deschamps insisting Les Bleus can adapt as Kylian Mbappé leads a red-hot attack. Paraguay arrive as underdogs after knocking out Germany, hoping resilience and tactical discipline can counter France’s pace and tournament momentum.
Heatwave casts shadow over France vs Paraguay World Cup last-16
The match in Philadelphia is set to kick off at 5:00 pm local time under an extreme-heat advisory, forcing both teams to factor conditions into preparation and in-game management. The weather is a genuine variable — one that can blunt high-tempo play and test fitness and squad depth.
Why the heat matters
High temperatures at Lincoln Financial Field increase the risk of fatigue and cramping, particularly late in the match. Coaches must balance intensity in the opening stages with rotation and hydration strategies. For a France side that has played four competitive matches already, recovery and substitution timing are as important as tactics.
France’s form and selection — Deschamps’s measured confidence
Didier Deschamps has downplayed alarm despite the heat, stressing preparation and adaptability. France have been the tournament’s standout side, winning all four games and scoring 13 goals. Kylian Mbappé’s six strikes make him the obvious focal point, but the team’s collective attacking balance has been equally impressive.
Squad status and tactical note
France are almost fully fit aside from the sidelined Marcus Thuram. That absence matters less given the depth in attack and Deschamps’s tendency to manage minutes across a talented roster. Expect France to control possession, use Mbappé’s explosive runs to stretch Paraguay, and lean on midfield rotation to preserve legs in the heat.
Paraguay’s path and underdog threat
Gustavo Alfaro’s Paraguay arrive with momentum from a dramatic penalty shootout win over Germany and a reputation for resilience. Ranked well below France in FIFA standings, they nevertheless carry belief from recent results and a pragmatic tactical approach suited to uncomfortable conditions.
How Paraguay can make this game competitive
Paraguay will likely compact the midfield, force France wide, and look to frustrate with disciplined defending and quick counters or set-piece opportunities. Their appetite for physical encounters and mental toughness from the Germany win gives them a clear blueprint: slow the tempo, protect shape, and hit on transitions.
Key players to watch
Kylian Mbappé — France’s primary match-winner, capable of breaking games open with individual brilliance. Didier Deschamps — his rotation and substitutions will dictate whether France can sustain intensity in heat. Gustavo Alfaro — his tactical choices will determine if Paraguay can stifle France’s attack and create enough moments to upset.
What this result would mean
A France victory would reinforce them as tournament favorites, validating depth and adaptability under adverse conditions. A Paraguay upset would be seismic: it would mark only their second World Cup quarter-final and underline how knockout football rewards organization and nerve over star power.
Looking ahead
Expect a game where environmental management is as critical as tactics. Coaches who adjust tempo, use substitutions intelligently and prioritize recovery will have an edge.
Extreme heat in Philadelphia puts France-Paraguay World Cup tie under scrutiny as FIFA safety protocols are tested
This contest is a reminder that in the World Cup knockout stages, context — form, conditions and mental resilience — often decides outcomes as much as raw talent.
